subject: Human Resources
title: Growth and Development Opportunities
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Growth and Development Opportunities.

hook.text: LinkedIn's data: the number one reason people leave jobs is lack of career growth. Not money. Growth.

buildup.text: Development includes promotions, stretch assignments, mentoring, training, and lateral moves that build new skills.

discovery.text: Employees who see a clear growth path are 20% more likely to still be at their company in one year.

twist.text: Growth doesn't always mean climbing up. Lateral moves, skill-building, and new challenges engage without a title change.

climax.text: The best managers have career conversations regularly—not just during annual reviews when it's too late.

punchline.text: If people can't grow here, they'll grow somewhere else.

quiz.question: According to LinkedIn, why do most people leave their jobs?
